description_long: This is a weathered wooden barrel with a cylindrical, bulging shape and reinforced by five evenly spaced, rusted metal hoops encircling its body. The wood staves are dark brown with visible grain, rough texture, and patches of green moss or algae, suggesting prolonged outdoor exposure. The top and bottom are flat and made of the same wood, with the top surface showing clear wood grain and some unevenness. The metal hoops are slightly corroded, adding to the aged appearance and providing structural integrity. There are no visible handles, spouts, or openings aside from the closed top. The overall look is rustic and utilitarian, suitable for storage or decorative use in urban outdoor settings.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with rusted metal hoops, dark wood, and green moss patches, giving it a rustic, aged appearance.
description_view_0: Front view shows the bulging wooden staves, five rusted metal hoops, and a flat, textured top with visible moss streaks.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the curvature of the barrel and the uneven wood grain, with metal hoops and moss more prominent on the left.
description_view_2: Another side view with slightly different wood grain patterns and moss distribution, metal hoops remain consistent.
description_view_3: Back view displays the same structural features, with the wood texture and moss appearing more concentrated on the right side.
